title: Choose the index type that fits the predicate
description: Match btree, gin, gist, or brin to the query shape rather than defaulting every index to btree.
body: Use when adding an index for a specific access pattern. Do not put a btree on everything. Actions: use btree for equality and range on scalar columns; gin for containment over jsonb, arrays, and full-text (tsvector); gist for geometric/range overlap and nearest-neighbor; brin for huge, naturally-ordered tables (time-series) where a tiny index over block ranges suffices. Verify with EXPLAIN that the planner actually uses it. Validation signals include the intended index appearing in the plan and a measured latency drop on the target query. Risk boundary: a gin index speeds reads but slows writes and bloats; on a write-heavy table, confirm the write cost is acceptable before committing.
